title: Habenaria hystrix
taxonomy: Habenaria hystrix is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. Specifically, it is a genus of orchids that belongs to the subfamily Orchidoideae. The subfamily Orchidoideae is further divided into tribes, with Habenaria hystrix being part of the tribe Orchideae. This tribe comprises a large number of orchid species found worldwide, many of which are terrestrial or epilithic. The exact classification of Habenaria hystrix may be subject to change as taxonomic studies continue.
uses: Habenaria hystrix has been used in traditional medicine due to its reported properties and effects. Its root and aerial parts have been used to treat various health conditions. Further research is needed to confirm its medicinal uses and potential benefits.

intro: Habenaria hystrix, commonly known as tiger orchid, is a species of orchid that belongs to the genus Habenaria. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Habenaria hystrix include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Habenaria hystrix is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of tinctures or extracts, making it a valuable resource in folk medicine.
